question.content: Determine the maximum recombination fraction between any two loci using the linked, independent-assortment, and unlinked scenarios in the figure. Output exactly one expression token (for example 1/3), plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no sentence, no equality sign, no chained equalities, and no extra text. Answer format: a simplest fraction such as a/b; write only the fraction itself, with no explanatory text, units, or equals sign.

step_1.content: Recombination fraction between two loci cannot exceed one half.
step_1.key_point: The maximum is capped by random assortment.

step_3.content: Independent assortment gives four equiprobable gamete classes.
step_3.key_point: Recombinant classes and parental classes are balanced.

step_4.content: Odd and even crossover parity can make distant loci appear unlinked overall.
step_4.key_point: Odd parity yields recombinant type and even parity yields parental type.

step_6.content: Across all cases, the maximal recombination fraction is one half.
step_6.key_point: The limiting value is reached in unlinked behavior.
